title: S-Dich exhaustive residual split contract
description: Specifies a state-measurable exhaustive split of the residual class C(B), where each alternative names its closing certificate, payload constructor, next CT tactic, or audit result. The record operationalizes the S-Dich exhaustive-split lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Dich`.

residualClass.description: Required mathematical definition of the exact residual class C(B) being partitioned, including the inherited H_0, E, I, R, V, Q, and A entries and any ledger, budget, payload, or certificate conditions active at this node.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose characterization of the residual class, with all active branch-state, vocabulary, ledger, budget, payload, certificate, and tactic-signature conditions.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX predicate defining the same residual class.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used for the residual predicate.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable definition of the residual class.

alternatives.description: Required list of at least two alternatives whose union is the residual class C(B). Each entry must be decidable from recorded branch-state data and must identify the unique framework destination responsible for it.

items.description: One branch alternative in the exhaustive split.

condition.description: Required mathematical guard for this alternative. It must include the exact state-measurable predicate, structural condition, ledger/budget comparison, payload trigger, certificate precondition, or negation used in the framework branch.

informal.description: Required nonempty prose statement of the branch condition and the objects to which it applies.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX rendering of the exact branch guard, such as a residual predicate, ledger inequality, budget-functional sign check, rank comparison, finite-table membership test, or trigger condition.

formalLanguage.description: Optional name and version of the formal language used to encode this guard.

formal.description: Optional machine-checkable predicate for this branch condition.

measurableFromState.description: Required literal `true`, asserting that the guard is determined by fields already recorded in the branch state and introduces no unrecorded choice or oracle.

feeds.description: Required routing target that takes responsibility for this alternative; it must correspond to an actual node, C1-C5 certificate, P_{i->j} payload, scope audit, or admission-rejection interface in the branch tree.

kind.description: Required destination category: a continuing `node`, terminal `certificate`, typed `payload`, `scope_audit`, or pre-execution `audit_reject`.

reference.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the destination, such as a proof-flow node, labelled result, certificate record, or payload consumed by a declared tactic.

pairwiseDisjoint.description: Optional declaration of whether no residual can satisfy two alternatives. Set to `true` only when disjointness is proved; complementary guards normally satisfy this, while a cover with ordered precedence may legitimately set `false`.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list proving that the alternatives cover the residual class and that every guard and route is valid.

items.description: One evidence record supporting exhaustiveness, disjointness, measurability, or routing.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for a case-analysis lemma, S-Dich record, dependency-table row, branch-tree node, payload route, certificate record, or audit entry establishing the split.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a finite case table, program, or formal artifact.

notes.description: Optional note stating which coverage, disjointness, or route obligation the evidence discharges.
